titleStmt.title: SII 15.493: original edition by P. B. Desai, Dines Chandra Sircar and G. S. Gai
No. 493. (B.K. No. 64 of 1927-28.) HAITĀPURA, MUNDARGI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

summary.p: On a pillar built into the wall of the Māruti temple.

It states that Bhagibe, wife of Bijja of the Chālukya lineage had a tank built below a hill, between Kōvuṟūr and Mōṟivaḷavu. The record describes the Chālukya family as belonging to the Brahma-Kshatriya community and gives the genealogy of Bijja as follows:—Baddiyamma, his son Maḍidāra, his son Gōkaṇa, his son Kattiyara, his son Gōvaṇṇa, his son Rāja, his son Bijja.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti brahmakṣatriya caḷukyakuḷānva
yado puṭṭade puṭṭida
baddiyammanā baddiyammana
maga maḍidāranā maḍidārana ma
ga gōkaṇadēvaṁ gōkaṇadēvi(va)
na maga kattiyara kattiya
rana maga gōvaṇnaṁ gōvaṇnana
magaṁ rājaṁ rājana magaṁ svasti
samadhigata paṁcamahā
śabda sāmanta biruda
ra birudara murutti
sarivōdharabbeyāṇmaṁ
vikramagaḷḷaṁ śrīmatbijja
na manōnaya
navallabha Anavarata śaha
śra bhōgabhāgini dvitīyalakṣmī
ssamāneyarappa bha
gibe śivana dāsi māhēśva
ra pakṣapāti kōvuṟūra mō
ṟivaḷavina naḍuvaṇa dēsa
beṭṭada keṟagondu keṟeyaThe
Digital edition of SII 15.493 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
